Title: Contact Us form does not enforce required fields
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Navigate to /contact_us
2. Leave Name Email Subject and Message fields blank
3. Click Submit
Expected Result: Browser should block submission of empty form (HTML5 required attribute) so users do not submit blank enquiries
Actual Result: The Name and Subject fields are missing the 'required' attribute. The form attempts to submit with empty values and only the Email field's type-based validation is enforced — Name and Subject can be submitted as empty strings. Verified via JS: input[name='name'].required === false

Title: Hover-only Add-to-cart on product card requires JS hover to be visible on desktop
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Navigate to /products
2. Locate the first product card without hovering
3. Attempt to click 'Add to cart' on the card
Expected Result: 'Add to cart' control should be reachable by keyboard / discoverable without hover for accessibility (WCAG 2.4.7 / 2.1.1)
Actual Result: The Add-to-cart button on listing cards is in an .overlay-content that is only made visible on hover. There is a duplicate '.productinfo .add-to-cart' that works, but the overlay variant is invisible to keyboard-only users and assistive tech

Title: Console errors on initial page load
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open browser DevTools Console
2. Navigate to https://automationexercise.com
3. Observe the console
Expected Result: Page should load without JavaScript errors
Actual Result: 3-5 console errors are emitted on every page load (advertising / network related). Captured by Playwright on each goto. While the site still functions these errors pollute logs and may mask real issues

Title: Duplicate success-message DOM containers (subscribe + contact)
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Navigate to home
2. Inspect the DOM for elements with class .alert-success or id #success-subscribe
3. Also navigate to /contact_us
Expected Result: Each success message container should be unique per feature to keep selectors stable
Actual Result: Both the home page subscription and the contact form render success banners that share class names and live in containers using the same id (#success-subscribe). This caused Playwright strict-mode locator collisions during automation and indicates duplicate IDs in the DOM (invalid HTML)
